revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 掛木古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kakegi_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|250px]] |所属 = |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触323 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|48|26.87|N|129|42|5.99|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline|name=掛木古墳}} |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 南北22.5m・東西18.0m
（推定復元：直径28-30m） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（3室）
（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末頃
（[[7世紀]]前半頃まで追葬） |被葬者 = |出土品 = 須恵器・土師器坏・銅鏡片・金環・鉄製品 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = 家形石棺（壱岐市指定文化財） |特記事項 = }} '''掛木古墳'''（かけぎこふん）は、壱岐市勝本町布気触にある古墳。形状は円墳。 本古墳は[[1989年]]（平成元年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされている{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘は円形で、現在は南北22.5メートル・東西18.0メートルを測るが、元々は直径約28-30メートルであったと推定される{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は複室構造の両袖式横穴式石室で、玄室・中室・前室の3室と羨道で構成され、南方に開口する{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。その規模は次の通り{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 :* 石室全長：13.6メートル :* 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ3メートル :* 中室：長さ3.0メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ1.5メートル 玄室には凝灰岩製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]（刳抜式は壱岐唯一[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/237 掛木古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}}）が据えられ、その規模は長さ1.9メートル、幅0.85メートル、高さ0.7メートル{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。ただし屋根蓋石の半分以上は欠損する{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。本古墳からの出土品としては、須恵器・土師器坏・銅鏡片・金環・鉄製品が見つかっている{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 この掛木古墳の築造時期は6世紀末頃、追葬は7世紀前半頃までと推定される。家形石棺は壱岐市指定有形文化財に指定されている{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}}。 ファイル:Kakegi kofun.jpg|掛木古墳外観 ファイル:Kakegi kofun sekishitsu1.jpg|墓道 ファイル:Kakegi kofun sekishitsu2.jpg|羨道 ファイル:Kakegi kofun genshitsu.jpg|玄室と石棺

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 掛木古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kakegi_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触323 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 26.87 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 6.00 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 南北22.5m・東西18.0m
（推定復元：直径28-30m） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = 銅鏡片・金環・鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末頃 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 家形石棺（壱岐市指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 掛木古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''掛木古墳'''（かけぎこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。家形石棺は壱岐市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1989年]]（平成元年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされている{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘は円形で、現在は南北22.5メートル・東西18.0メートルを測るが、元々は直径約28-30メートルであったと推定される{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は複室構造の両袖式横穴式石室で、玄室・中室・前室の3室と羨道で構成され、南方に開口する{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。その規模は次の通り{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：13.6メートル * 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ3メートル * 中室：長さ3.0メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ1.5メートル 玄室には凝灰岩製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]（刳抜式は壱岐唯一[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/237 掛木古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}}）が据えられ、その規模は長さ1.9メートル、幅0.85メートル、高さ0.7メートル{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。ただし屋根蓋石の半分以上は欠損する{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。本古墳からの出土品としては、須恵器・土師器坏・銅鏡片・金環・鉄製品が見つかっている{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は6世紀末頃、追葬は7世紀前半頃までと推定される。家形石棺は壱岐市指定有形文化財に指定されている{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}}。 Kakegi kofun.jpg|掛木古墳外観 Kakegi kofun sekishitsu1.jpg|墓道 Kakegi kofun sekishitsu2.jpg|羨道 Kakegi kofun genshitsu.jpg|玄室と石棺 == 文化財 == === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 掛木古墳の家形石棺（石造物）{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}} == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=掛木古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|volume=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:かけきこふん}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]]

（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = 銅鏡片・金環・鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末頃 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 家形石棺（壱岐市指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 掛木古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''掛木古墳'''（かけぎこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触にある[[古墳]]。 形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。家形石棺は壱岐市指定[[有形文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1989年]]（平成元年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされている{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘は円形で、現在は南北22.5メートル・東西18.0メートルを測るが、元々は直径約28-30メートルであったと推定される{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は複室構造の両袖式横穴式石室で、玄室・中室・前室の3室と羨道で構成され、南方に開口する{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。その規模は次の通り{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：13.6メートル * 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ3メートル * 中室：長さ3.0メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ1.5メートル 玄室には凝灰岩製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]（刳抜式は壱岐唯一[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/237 掛木古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}}）が据えられ、その規模は長さ1.9メートル、幅0.85メートル、高さ0.7メートル{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。ただし屋根蓋石の半分以上は欠損する{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。本古墳からの出土品としては、須恵器・土師器坏・銅鏡片・金環・鉄製品が見つかっている{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は6世紀末頃、追葬は7世紀前半頃までと推定される。家形石棺は壱岐市指定有形文化財に指定されている{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}}。 Kakegi kofun.jpg|掛木古墳外観 Kakegi kofun sekishitsu1.jpg|墓道 Kakegi kofun sekishitsu2.jpg|羨道 Kakegi kofun genshitsu.jpg|玄室と石棺 == 文化財 == === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 掛木古墳の家形石棺（石造物）{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}} == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=掛木古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|volume=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - }} '''掛木古墳'''（かけぎこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触にある[[古墳]]。 形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。家形石棺は壱岐市指定[[有形文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1989年]]（平成元年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされている{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘は円形で、現在は南北22.5メートル・東西18.0メートルを測るが、元々は直径約28-30メートルであったと推定される{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は複室構造の両袖式横穴式石室で、玄室・中室・前室の3室と羨道で構成され、南方に開口する{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。その規模は次の通り{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：13.6メートル * 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ3メートル * 中室：長さ3.0メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ1.5メートル 玄室には凝灰岩製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]（刳抜式は壱岐唯一[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/237 掛木古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}}）が据えられ、その規模は長さ1.9メートル、幅0.85メートル、高さ0.7メートル{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。ただし屋根蓋石の半分以上は欠損する{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。本古墳からの出土品としては、須恵器・土師器坏・銅鏡片・金環・鉄製品が見つかっている{{Sfn|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は6世紀末頃、追葬は7世紀前半頃までと推定される。家形石棺は壱岐市指定有形文化財に指定されている{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}}。 Kakegi kofun.jpg|掛木古墳外観 Kakegi kofun sekishitsu1.jpg|墓道 Kakegi kofun sekishitsu2.jpg|羨道 Kakegi kofun genshitsu.jpg|玄室と石棺 == 文化財 == === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 掛木古墳の家形石棺（石造物）{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}} == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=掛木古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|volume=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:かけきこふん}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: 全体加筆修正、画像追加と更新。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 掛木古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触（字掛木） |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 26.75 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 6.08 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径28-30m（推定復元）
高さ6.8m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = 銅鏡・金環・鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 家形石棺（壱岐市指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 掛木古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''掛木古墳'''（かけぎこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+史跡「[[壱岐古墳群]]」6基 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !築造時期 |- |[[対馬塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長63m||6c後半 |- |[[双六古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長91m||6c後半 |- |[[笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|笹塚古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径66m||6c後半-末 |- |[[兵瀬古墳]]||円墳||直径54m||6c後半-末 |- |'''掛木古墳'''||円墳||直径28-30m||6c後半-末 |- |[[鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|鬼の窟古墳]]||円墳||直径45m||6c後半-末 |}
壱岐島中央部の丘陵平坦面縁辺部（標高101メートル）に築造された大型円墳である。[[1989年]]度（[[平成]]元年度）に墳丘測量・石室実測・玄室の一部発掘調査が実施されている。 墳丘の周囲は大きく削平され、現在は南北22.5メートル・東西18メートル・高さ6.8メートルを測るが、元の墳形は円形で、直径28-30m程度と推定される{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・中室・前室・羨道からなる3室構造で、石室全長13.6メートルを測る大型石室であり、石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の巨石が使用される。石室の玄室内には、壱岐島では唯一となる[[凝灰岩]]製刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据える。石室内の部分発掘調査では、副葬品として銅鏡片・金環・鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]が検出されている。築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末頃と推定され、[[7世紀]]中頃までの追葬が想定される{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 古墳域は[[2009年]]（平成21年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）、家形石棺は勝本町指定有形文化財（現在は壱岐市指定有形文化財）に指定されている。現在は史跡整備のうえで公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[文久]]元年（[[1861年]]）完成の『壱岐名勝図誌』に記載（当時には開口）。 * [[1989年]]度（[[平成]]元年度）、県内古墳詳細分布調査に伴う墳丘測量・石室実測・玄室一部発掘調査（長崎県教育委員会、[[1992年]]に報告）{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 * [[2009年]]（平成21年）2月12日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・中室・前室・羨道からなる3室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 * 石室全長：13.6メートル * 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、 * 中室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ1.5メートル * 前室：長さ3.2メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.7メートル 石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の自然石の巨石が使用される。玄室の平面形は正方形に近いややいびつな長方形、中室・前室は長方形である。玄室は奥壁・腰石に巨石を据え、若干持ち送り気味に積み、天井石との隙間に板状の石を詰める。天井石は1枚。中室・前室は巨石の腰石の上に羨門側から天井石を重ねながら積み、袖石と天井石の隙間に礫を詰める。玄室入り口・前室入り口には框石を置く。前室前面の東側には大石を据えて短い羨道を形成するが、西側の石は日露戦争の戦没者墓石として使用するために失われている（現在は復元）{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 玄室内には奥壁に接する形で地元産の[[凝灰岩]]製刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据える。現在は蓋石の半分以上を欠失し、棺身の西側小口に立て掛けられる。底厚の石棺で、外法は長さ1.90メートル・幅0.95メートル・高さ0.70メートル、内法は長さ1.50メートル・幅0.46メートル・高さ0.30メートル、蓋石は屋根形で残存長0.87メートル・幅0.94メートル・高さ0.39メートル・内側刳抜10センチメートルを測る。この石棺は、江戸時代の『壱岐名勝図誌』にも石櫃として描かれている{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。 石室内からは、1989年度（平成元年度）の部分発掘調査の際に銅鏡片（仿製獣帯鏡）・金環・鉄釘・馬具（轡）・[[須恵器]]（坏蓋・坏身・器台）・[[土師器]]（坏：畿内系土師器か）が検出されている。鉄釘の出土は木棺の使用を示唆する。また後世の遺物として、13世紀後半-14前半代の青磁片や近世陶磁器・寛永通宝などがある{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 Kakegi Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Kakegi Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Kakegi Kofun, chushitsu-2.jpg|中室（開口部方向） Kakegi Kofun, chushitsu-1.jpg|中室（玄室方向） Kakegi Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg|前室（開口部方向） Kakegi Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|前室（玄室方向） Kakegi Kofun, entrance.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|墳丘・開口部 == 文化財 == === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 掛木古墳の家形石棺（石造物）{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}} == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=掛木古墳|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|volume=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=掛木古墳|title=壱岐の島の古墳群～現状調査|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=掛木古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:かけきこふん}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]]

玄室内には奥壁に接する形で地元産の[[凝灰岩]]製刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据える。現在は蓋石の半分以上を欠失し、棺身の西側小口に立て掛けられる。底厚の石棺で、外法は長さ1.90メートル・幅0.95メートル・高さ0.70メートル、内法は長さ1.50メートル・幅0.46メートル・高さ0.30メートル、蓋石は屋根形で残存長0.87メートル・幅0.94メートル・高さ0.39メートル・内側刳抜10センチメートルを測る。この石棺は、江戸時代の『壱岐名勝図誌』にも石櫃として描かれている{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。 石室内からは、1989年度（平成元年度）の部分発掘調査の際に銅鏡片（仿製獣帯鏡）・金環・鉄釘・馬具（轡）・[[須恵器]]（坏蓋・坏身・器台）・[[土師器]]（坏：畿内系土師器か）が検出されている。鉄釘の出土は木棺の使用を示唆する。また後世の遺物として、13世紀後半-14前半代の青磁片や近世陶磁器・寛永通宝などがある{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 Kakegi Kofun, {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 掛木古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触（字掛木） |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 26.75 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 6.08 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径28-30m（推定復元）
高さ6.8m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = 銅鏡・金環・鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 家形石棺（壱岐市指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 掛木古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''掛木古墳'''（かけぎこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。家形石棺は壱岐市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+史跡「[[壱岐古墳群]]」6基 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !築造時期 |- |[[対馬塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長63m||6c後半 |- |[[双六古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長91m||6c後半 |- |[[笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|笹塚古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径66m||6c後半-末 |- |[[兵瀬古墳]]||円墳||直径54m||6c後半-末 |- |'''掛木古墳'''||円墳||直径28-30m||6c後半-末 |- |[[鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|鬼の窟古墳]]||円墳||直径45m||6c後半-末 |}
壱岐島中央部の丘陵平坦面縁辺部（標高101メートル）に築造された大型円墳である。[[1989年]]度（[[平成]]元年度）に墳丘測量・石室実測・石室内一部発掘調査が実施されている。 墳丘の周囲は大きく削平され、現在は南北22.5メートル・東西18メートル・高さ6.8メートルを測るが、元の墳形は円形で、直径28-30m程度と推定される{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・中室・前室・羨道からなる3室構造で、石室全長13.6メートルを測る大型石室であり、石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の巨石が使用される。石室の玄室内には、壱岐島では唯一となる[[凝灰岩]]製刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据える。石室内の部分発掘調査では、副葬品として銅鏡片・金環・鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]が検出されている。築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末頃と推定され、[[7世紀]]中頃までの追葬が想定される{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 古墳域は[[2009年]]（平成21年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）、家形石棺は勝本町指定有形文化財（現在は壱岐市指定有形文化財）に指定されている。現在は史跡整備のうえで公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[文久]]元年（[[1861年]]）完成の『壱岐名勝図誌』に記載（当時には開口）。 * [[1989年]]度（[[平成]]元年度）、県内古墳詳細分布調査に伴う墳丘測量・石室実測・石室内一部発掘調査（長崎県教育委員会、[[1992年]]に報告）{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 * [[2009年]]（平成21年）2月12日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・中室・前室・羨道からなる3室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 * 石室全長：13.6メートル * 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、 * 中室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ1.5メートル * 前室：長さ3.2メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.7メートル 石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の自然石の巨石が使用される。玄室の平面形は正方形に近いややいびつな長方形、中室・前室は長方形である。玄室は奥壁・腰石に巨石を据え、若干持ち送り気味に積み、天井石との隙間に板状の石を詰める。天井石は1枚。中室・前室は巨石の腰石の上に羨門側から天井石を重ねながら積み、袖石と天井石の隙間に礫を詰める。玄室入り口・前室入り口には框石を置く。前室前面の東側には大石を据えて短い羨道を形成するが、西側の石は日露戦争の戦没者墓石として使用するために失われている（現在は復元）{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 玄室内には奥壁に接する形で地元産の[[凝灰岩]]製刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据える。現在は蓋石の半分以上を欠失し、棺身の西側小口に立て掛けられる。底厚の石棺で、外法は長さ1.90メートル・幅0.95メートル・高さ0.70メートル、内法は長さ1.50メートル・幅0.46メートル・高さ0.30メートル、蓋石は屋根形で残存長0.87メートル・幅0.94メートル・高さ0.39メートル・内側刳抜10センチメートルを測る。この石棺は、江戸時代の『壱岐名勝図誌』にも石櫃として描かれている{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。 石室内からは、1989年度（平成元年度）の部分発掘調査の際に銅鏡片（仿製獣帯鏡）・金環・鉄釘・馬具（轡）・[[須恵器]]（坏蓋・坏身・器台）・[[土師器]]（坏：畿内系土師器か）が検出されている。鉄釘の出土は木棺の使用を示唆する。また後世の遺物として、13世紀後半-14前半代の青磁片や近世陶磁器・寛永通宝などがある{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 Kakegi Kofun, 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・中室・前室・羨道からなる3室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 * 石室全長：13.6メートル * 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、 * 中室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ1.5メートル * 前室：長さ3.2メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.7メートル 石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の自然石の巨石が使用される。玄室の平面形は正方形に近いややいびつな長方形、中室・前室は長方形である。玄室は奥壁・腰石に巨石を据え、若干持ち送り気味に積み、天井石との隙間に板状の石を詰める。天井石は1枚。中室・前室は巨石の腰石の上に羨門側から天井石を重ねながら積み、袖石と天井石の隙間に礫を詰める。玄室入り口・前室入り口には框石を置く。前室前面の東側には大石を据えて短い羨道を形成するが、西側の石は日露戦争の戦没者墓石として使用するために失われている（現在は復元）{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 玄室内には奥壁に接する形で地元産の[[凝灰岩]]製刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据える。現在は蓋石の半分以上を欠失し、棺身の西側小口に立て掛けられる。底厚の石棺で、外法は長さ1.90メートル・幅0.95メートル・高さ0.70メートル、内法は長さ1.50メートル・幅0.46メートル・高さ0.30メートル、蓋石は屋根形で残存長0.87メートル・幅0.94メートル・高さ0.39メートル・内側刳抜10センチメートルを測る。この石棺は、江戸時代の『壱岐名勝図誌』にも石櫃として描かれている{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。 石室内からは、1989年度（平成元年度）の部分発掘調査の際に銅鏡片（仿製獣帯鏡）・金環・鉄釘・馬具（轡）・[[須恵器]]（坏蓋・坏身・器台）・[[土師器]]（坏：畿内系土師器か）が検出されている。鉄釘の出土は木棺の使用を示唆する。また後世の遺物として、13世紀後半-14前半代の青磁片や近世陶磁器・寛永通宝などがある{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 Kakegi Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Kakegi Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Kakegi Kofun, chushitsu-2.jpg|中室（開口部方向） Kakegi Kofun, chushitsu-1.jpg|中室（玄室方向） Kakegi Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg|前室（開口部方向） Kakegi Kofun, {{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | Image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Kakegi Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the burial mound, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Designated on ... == 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 == Iki Kofun Group * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. {{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | Image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Kakegi Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the burial mound, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:転送]] [[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Kakegi Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the burial mound, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:redirect]] [[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. {{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | Image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Kakegi Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the burial mound, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:redirect]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. {{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | Image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Kakegi Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the burial mound, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Kakegi Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the burial mound, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Kakegi Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the burial mound, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}.
