{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | Name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | image caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Kakegi Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the Kofuns that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | Name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | image caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | Special Notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label Position = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the Kofuns that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | Name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | image caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the Kofuns that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "other_designation of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. (with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun part_of" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the Kofuns that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun part_of|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun part_of." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in shape, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun part_ofs of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun part_of] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "other_designation of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun part_of]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun part_of]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-typed Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "other_designation of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | Name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | image caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | ISO = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the Kofuns that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Cultural Properties of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "other_designation of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Designated on ... == 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 == Iki Kofun Group * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Designated on ... 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 [[ja:掛木古墳]] [[sn:Kakegi Kofun]][[sn:Kakegi Kofun]][[sn:Kakegi Kofun]][[sn:Kakegi Kofun]][[sn:Kakegi Kofun]][[sn:Kakegi Kofun]][[sn:Kakegi Kofun]][[sn:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[Category: 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the Kofuns that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == other_designation == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|other_designation of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "other_designation of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == other_designation == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|other_designation of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "other_designation of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | Name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | image caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | ISO = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the Kofuns that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == other_designation == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|other_designation of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "other_designation of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | image caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | ISO = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the Kofuns that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == other_designation == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|other_designation of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "other_designation of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == other_designation == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|other_designation of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "other_designation of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National other_designations of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | image caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside) | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron artifacts, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Person Buried = | other_designation = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = House-shaped stone coffin (designated cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the Kofuns that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was designated as a national {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is designated as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal dimensions of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. Artifacts discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later artifacts such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == other_designation == === Tangible Cultural Property of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|other_designation of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "other_designation of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:National other_designations of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[en:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] * [[Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:nowiki former opening]]* {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and [[Category:nowiki former opening]]{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}.
