(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped stone coffin (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft|12=draft}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft|12=draft}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=draft|12=draft}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped stone coffin (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|12=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=draft}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=draft}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=draft}}. revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 掛木古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触（字掛木） |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 26.75 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 6.08 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径28-30m（推定復元）
高さ6.8m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = 銅鏡・金環・鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 家形石棺（壱岐市指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 掛木古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''掛木古墳'''（かけぎこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町布気触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。家形石棺は壱岐市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+史跡「[[壱岐古墳群]]」6基 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !築造時期 |- |[[対馬塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長63m||6c後半 |- |[[双六古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長91m||6c後半 |- |[[笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|笹塚古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径66m||6c後半-末 |- |[[兵瀬古墳]]||円墳||直径54m||6c後半-末 |- |'''掛木古墳'''||円墳||直径28-30m||6c後半-末 |- |[[鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|鬼の窟古墳]]||円墳||直径45m||6c後半-末 |}
壱岐島中央部の丘陵平坦面縁辺部（標高101メートル）に築造された大型円墳である。[[1989年]]度（[[平成]]元年度）に墳丘測量・石室実測・石室内一部発掘調査が実施されている。 墳丘の周囲は大きく削平され、現在は南北22.5メートル・東西18メートル・高さ6.8メートルを測るが、元の墳形は円形で、直径28-30m程度と推定される{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・中室・前室・羨道からなる3室構造で、石室全長13.6メートルを測る大型石室であり、石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の巨石が使用される。石室の玄室内には、壱岐島では唯一となる[[凝灰岩]]製刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据える。石室内の部分発掘調査では、副葬品として銅鏡片・金環・鉄製品・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]が検出されている。築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末頃と推定され、[[7世紀]]中頃までの追葬が想定される{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 古墳域は[[2009年]]（平成21年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）、家形石棺は勝本町指定有形文化財（現在は壱岐市指定有形文化財）に指定されている。現在は史跡整備のうえで公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[文久]]元年（[[1861年]]）完成の『壱岐名勝図誌』に記載（当時には開口）。 * [[1989年]]度（[[平成]]元年度）、県内古墳詳細分布調査に伴う墳丘測量・石室実測・石室内一部発掘調査（長崎県教育委員会、[[1992年]]に報告）{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 * [[2009年]]（平成21年）2月12日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南西方向に開口する。玄室・中室・前室・羨道からなる3室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 * 石室全長：13.6メートル * 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、 * 中室：長さ3メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ1.5メートル * 前室：長さ3.2メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.7メートル 石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の自然石の巨石が使用される。玄室の平面形は正方形に近いややいびつな長方形、中室・前室は長方形である。玄室は奥壁・腰石に巨石を据え、若干持ち送り気味に積み、天井石との隙間に板状の石を詰める。天井石は1枚。中室・前室は巨石の腰石の上に羨門側から天井石を重ねながら積み、袖石と天井石の隙間に礫を詰める。玄室入り口・前室入り口には框石を置く。前室前面の東側には大石を据えて短い羨道を形成するが、西側の石は日露戦争の戦没者墓石として使用するために失われている（現在は復元）{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 玄室内には奥壁に接する形で地元産の[[凝灰岩]]製刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据える。現在は蓋石の半分以上を欠失し、棺身の西側小口に立て掛けられる。底厚の石棺で、外法は長さ1.90メートル・幅0.95メートル・高さ0.70メートル、内法は長さ1.50メートル・幅0.46メートル・高さ0.30メートル、蓋石は屋根形で残存長0.87メートル・幅0.94メートル・高さ0.39メートル・内側刳抜10センチメートルを測る。この石棺は、江戸時代の『壱岐名勝図誌』にも石櫃として描かれている{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。 石室内からは、1989年度（平成元年度）の部分発掘調査の際に銅鏡片（仿製獣帯鏡）・金環・鉄釘・馬具（轡）・[[須恵器]]（坏蓋・坏身・器台）・[[土師器]]（坏：畿内系土師器か）が検出されている。鉄釘の出土は木棺の使用を示唆する。また後世の遺物として、13世紀後半-14前半代の青磁片や近世陶磁器・寛永通宝などがある{{Sfn|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}。 Kakegi Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Kakegi Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Kakegi Kofun, chushitsu-2.jpg|中室（開口部方向） Kakegi Kofun, chushitsu-1.jpg|中室（玄室方向） Kakegi Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg|前室（開口部方向） Kakegi Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|前室（玄室方向） Kakegi Kofun, entrance.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|墳丘・開口部 == 文化財 == === 壱岐市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 掛木古墳の家形石棺（石造物）{{Sfn|壱岐市の文化財|2017|p=116}} == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=掛木古墳|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|series=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書|1992}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=掛木古墳|title=壱岐の島の古墳群～現状調査|series=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=掛木古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|掛木古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:かけきこふん}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]]

Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped stone coffin (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}}. Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped stone coffin (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft|12=draft}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|12=draft|12=draft}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=draft|12=draft}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=draft|12=draft}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped stone coffin (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tuff|qq=}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. Designated on ... == 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 == Iki Kofun Group * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City|qq=}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped stone coffin (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|ancient Kofun (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient Kofun (kofun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City|qq=}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}. The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tuff|qq=}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tuff|qq=}} carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. Designated on ... == 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 == Iki Kofun Group * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped stone coffin (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (lids, bowls, stands), and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. Designated on ... == 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 == Iki Kofun Group * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped stone coffin (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped stone coffin is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced [[:en:Tuff|tuff]] carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This stone coffin was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (lids, bowls, stands), and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}.
