[[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] {{infobox ancient site | name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped Sarcophagus (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This Sarcophagus was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. (with {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped Sarcophagus (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This Sarcophagus was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. (with {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped Sarcophagus (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This Sarcophagus was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] {{infobox ancient site | name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped Sarcophagus (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This Sarcophagus was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This Sarcophagus was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] {{infobox ancient site | name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped Sarcophagus (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This Sarcophagus was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This Sarcophagus was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kakegi Kofun]][[ja:掛木古墳]] {{infobox ancient site | name = Kakegi Kofun (掛木古墳) | image = [[File:Kakegi Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}} | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 26.75 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 6.08 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 28–30 m (estimated restoration)
Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped Sarcophagus (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This Sarcophagus was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. This Sarcophagus was illustrated in the Edo-period "Iki Meisho Zushi"{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}{{Sfn|Kofun Groups of Iki Island|2012}}. burial goods discovered during the 1989 excavation include bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron nails, horse gear (bits), {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (lids, bowls, stands), and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. Height 6.8 m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Bronze mirror, gold ring, iron burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = House-shaped Sarcophagus (Historic site cultural property of Iki City) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Kakegi Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Kakegi Kofun''' (掛木古墳) is an {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|qid=Q861519|lt=Iki City|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Katsumoto Town, Fuke Fure (Aza Kakegi), {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == Overview == This large round kofun was constructed on the edge of a flat hilltop area in central Iki Island (elevation 101 meters). In 1989 (Heisei 1), a survey of the Kofun, precise measurements of the stone chamber, and partial excavations inside the chamber were conducted. The mound has been heavily eroded, currently measuring 22.5 m north-south, 18 m east-west, and 6.8 m in height. It is estimated that the original mound was circular with a diameter of about 28–30 m{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber, with a total length of 13.6 m. Large basalt stones were used as construction materials. Inside the main chamber, there is a unique locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}—the only one found on Iki Island. Excavations have uncovered burial goods such as bronze mirror fragments, gold rings, iron items, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The mound is estimated to have been constructed in the late 6th century, with subsequent burials likely continuing until the mid-7th century{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. The kofun was Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} in 2009 (Heisei 21) as part of the "Iki Kofun Group." The house-shaped Sarcophagus is Historic site as a tangible cultural property of Iki City. == burial == [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Kakegi Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south-southwest. It consists of three chambers: the main chamber, middle chamber, and antechamber. The area are as follows{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}: * Total length: 13.6 m * Main chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m * Middle chamber: Length 3 m, Width 2.6 m, Height 1.5 m * Antechamber: Length 3.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.7 m Large natural basalt stones were used in the construction. The main chamber is nearly square in type, with the middle and antechambers being rectangular. A single large ceiling stone covers the main chamber, with other chambers featuring stacked stones filled with gravel between gaps. The main chamber houses a locally sourced {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, placed against the back wall. The coffin is missing more than half of its lid, with the remaining part leaning against its western side. The coffin measures 1.9 m in length, 0.95 m in width, and 0.7 m in height externally, with internal area of 1.5 m by 0.46 m and 0.3 m deep. The lid is gabled, measuring 0.87 m in length, 0.94 m in width, and 0.39 m in height with a carved interior of 10 cm. The presence of iron nails suggests wooden coffins were used. Later burial goods such as celadon shards from the late 13th to early 14th centuries and Edo-period coins were also found{{Sfn|Detailed Survey Report of Kofun in the Prefecture|1992}}. == Historic site == === Tangible designation1 of Iki City === * House-shaped Stone Coffin of Kakegi Kofun (Stone Artifact){{Sfn|Historic site of Iki City|2017|p=116}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11429588}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kakegi Kofun}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Circular mound (north-south: 22.5 meters; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}}, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|1=Hyoze Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126604917}}, {{ill|Kakegi Kofun|ja|'''掛木古墳'''|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}.
